faq.title: Questions Homeowners Ask.
faq.intro: Straight answers about electrical work, permits, and what to expect when you hire a licensed contractor in the GTA.

items.question: Are you licensed and insured?
items.answer: Yes. Peace of Mind Electric holds a valid ECRA/ESA contractor licence and carries liability insurance on every job. Permits and inspections are coordinated as required — this is placeholder demo copy.

items.question: How much does a panel upgrade cost?
items.answer: Most residential panel upgrades in the GTA range from a few thousand dollars depending on amperage, meter base work, and whether the utility service needs upgrading. We provide a written quote after an on-site assessment.

items.question: Do I need a permit for electrical work?
items.answer: Most panel changes, service upgrades, new circuits, EV chargers, and generator installs require an ESA permit and inspection in Ontario. We handle permit filing and final inspection as part of the project scope.

items.question: Can you install an EV charger at my home?
items.answer: In most cases, yes — after we verify panel capacity and the run from your electrical service to the parking location. We install dedicated circuits, conduit, and charger mounts to code.

items.question: How do I know if my wiring needs to be replaced?
items.answer: Frequent breaker trips, flickering lights, warm outlets, aluminum or knob-and-tube wiring, and homes built before 1970 without updates are common signs. A licensed inspection is the safest way to know your options.

items.question: Do you offer emergency electrical service?
items.answer: We provide emergency response for existing clients. For new inquiries, contact us to discuss urgency — same-day options may be available depending on crew schedule and scope.

finalCta.headline: Ready for Peace of Mind?
